comment_text.sn35.27:1.3: The phrase “eye, sights, eye consciousness, and things knowable by eye consciousness” appears redundant, as “sights” (rūpa) are precisely what is “knowable by eye consciousness”. Where the phrase occurs elsewhere, rūpa may appear (MN 112:10.2, SN 35.65:2.1, SN 35.68:1.3) or it may not (MN 144:9.1, SN 35.87:7.1). Bodhi summarizes the commentary’s explanations, which he describes as “contrived” (Middle Length Discourses, note 1080, and Connected Discourses note 12 on book 4). At least one Chinese parallel omits rūpa here, thus avoiding the redundancy (MA 187 at T i 732c19). It is likely the original passage did not include rūpa and it was added in an old editorial oversight.

root_text.sn35.27:1.1: “Sabbaṁ, bhikkhave, anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.27:1.2: Kiñca, bhikkhave, sabbaṁ anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya?
root_text.sn35.27:1.3: Yañca, bhikkhave, cakkhu, ye ca rūpā, yañca cakkhuviññāṇaṁ, ye ca cakkhuviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā …pe…
root_text.sn35.27:1.4: yā ca jivhā, ye ca rasā, yañca jivhāviññāṇaṁ, ye ca jivhāviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā;
root_text.sn35.27:1.5: yo ca kāyo, ye ca phoṭṭhabbā, yañca kāyaviññāṇaṁ, ye ca kāyaviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā;
root_text.sn35.27:1.6: yo ca mano, ye ca dhammā, yañca manoviññāṇaṁ, ye ca manoviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā—
root_text.sn35.27:1.7: idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, sabbaṁ anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.27:2.1: Sabbaṁ, bhikkhave, abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.27:2.2: Kiñca, bhikkhave, sabbaṁ abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāya?
root_text.sn35.27:2.3: Yañca, bhikkhave, cakkhu, ye ca rūpā, yañca cakkhuviññāṇaṁ, ye ca cakkhuviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā …pe…
root_text.sn35.27:2.4: yā ca jivhā, ye ca rasā, yañca jivhāviññāṇaṁ, ye ca jivhāviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā;
root_text.sn35.27:2.5: yo ca kāyo, ye ca phoṭṭhabbā, yañca kāyaviññāṇaṁ, ye ca kāyaviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā;
root_text.sn35.27:2.6: yo ca mano, ye ca dhammā, yañca manoviññāṇaṁ, ye ca manoviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā—
root_text.sn35.27:2.7: idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, sabbaṁ abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāyā”ti.

translation_text.sn35.27:1.1: “Mendicants, without directly knowing and completely understanding the all, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.
translation_text.sn35.27:1.2: And what is the all, without directly knowing and completely understanding which, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering?
translation_text.sn35.27:1.3: The eye, sights, eye consciousness, and things knowable by eye consciousness.
translation_text.sn35.27:1.6: The mind, ideas, mind consciousness, and things knowable by mind consciousness.
translation_text.sn35.27:1.7: This is the all, without directly knowing and completely understanding which, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.
translation_text.sn35.27:2.1: By directly knowing and completely understanding the all, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering.
translation_text.sn35.27:2.2: And what is the all, directly knowing and completely understanding which, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering?
translation_text.sn35.27:2.3: The eye, sights, eye consciousness, and things knowable by eye consciousness.
translation_text.sn35.27:2.6: The mind, ideas, mind consciousness, and things knowable by mind consciousness.
translation_text.sn35.27:2.7: This is the all, directly knowing and completely understanding which, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering.”

reference_text.sn35.27:1.1: csp1ed13.251, csp2ed13.251, ms13S4_72, msdiv27, ndp14.18, pts-vp-pli4.19, sya18.23, vri26.18
